diagrams.title: Access Specifier Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows three concentric circles representing access levels from most restricted to least restricted
diagrams.purpose: Visualize that private is most restrictive, protected is intermediate, public is least restrictive

diagrams.title: Inheritance Access Control Table
diagrams.description: Matrix showing how each base class member type transforms under each inheritance type
diagrams.purpose: Quick reference for inheritance access transformation rules

diagrams.title: Constructor Destructor Flow
diagrams.description: Arrow diagram showing execution order: Base Constructor → Derived Constructor → ... → Derived Destructor → Base Destructor
diagrams.purpose: Remember that construction flows outward (base to derived) and destruction flows inward (derived to base)

diagrams.title: Is-a vs Has-a Relationships
diagrams.description: Two box diagrams: public inheritance (solid arrow 'is-a') and composition (hollow diamond 'has-a')
diagrams.purpose: Distinguish when to use inheritance vs composition based on relationship type
